page: Microsoft Azure EC48as_v5 is an ECASv5-family virtual machine configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory. Built on the x86_64 CPU architecture, this server utilizes AMD processors and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The memory-to-vCPU ratio is 8.0 GB per core, positioning the instance within the memory-optimized hardware class.

This instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached storage. It is Premium Storage capable, allowing connection to high-performance persistent disks. There are zero integrated GPUs and zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with the default configuration.

From a resource density perspective, the EC48as_v5 offers a high memory-to-core ratio, which provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require local temporary storage or hardware acceleration. The primary tradeoff is the complete reliance on remote storage volumes and the lack of local scratch space, which may introduce latency for disk-heavy operations.

The hardware configuration is designed for memory-intensive applications. Suitable workloads include relational databases, in-memory caching systems, large-scale data analytics, and enterprise applications requiring substantial RAM capacity without the need for local SSDs or GPU acceleration.
description: The Microsoft Azure EC48as_v5 is an ECASv5-family virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Powered by AMD processors and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance is classified as memory-optimized. It contains no local storage, relying entirely on external Premium Storage-capable volumes for disk operations, and includes no integrated GPUs. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as relational databases, in-memory caches, and data analytics. The lack of local storage and GPU accelerators represents a key architectural tradeoff, optimizing the instance for workloads where RAM capacity and dedicated CPU resources are prioritized over local disk speed and parallel graphics processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure EC48as_v5 is an ECASv5-family virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated AMD vCPUs, 384.0 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage capability, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring forty-eight dedicated cores and three hundred eighty-four gigabytes of RAM for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
